channel.title: Augmented Reality | TMU Multimedia Research Laboratory

item.title: Intelligent Scene Understanding for Mobile Augmented Reality
item.description:
Overview

This project develops machine learning algorithms for 3D object recognition using point cloud data captured from ARKit and ARCore frameworks, enabling intelligent scene understanding and collaborative augmented reality experiences on mobile devices. In partnership with AWE Company, the work targets real-world AR applications that can semantically understand their environment in real time.

Collaborators

  • AWE Company

Funding

  • NSERC Collaborative Research and Development Grant (CRD)

item.title: NSERC CRD Award: Intelligent Scene Understanding for Mobile AR
item.description:
Dr. Naimul Khan has been awarded a $90,000 NSERC Collaborative Research and Development Grant in partnership with AWE Company Ltd.

This one-year project will develop machine learning algorithms that leverage point cloud data from modern mobile AR libraries (ARKit/ARCore) to provide intelligent 3D scene understanding. The resulting scene understanding capabilities will further enable collaborative augmented reality experiences between multiple users sharing the same physical space.

item.title: NSERC CRD Award: 3D Object Recognition for Museum Exhibits
item.description:
Dr. Naimul Khan, Dr. Ling Guan, and Dr. Andy Ye have been awarded a $375,000 NSERC Collaborative Research and Development Grant in partnership with SimentIT Inc.

This two-year project will develop novel 3D object recognition and tracking algorithms for museum augmented reality exhibits. The technology will be deployed at the newly renovated Canada Science and Technology Museum in Ottawa, enabling visitors to experience interactive AR overlays on physical artifacts.

item.title: Augmented Reality Framework for Museum Exhibits
item.description:
Overview

In partnership with SimentIT and the Canadian Science and Technology Museum (CSTM) in Ottawa, this project developed a cloud-based mobile augmented reality platform for museum exhibits. The system enables real-time 3D object recognition and pose estimation under challenging and variable museum lighting conditions. The technology was deployed in CSTM’s “Artifact Alley”, where visitors experience a barrier-free and natural AR experience overlaid on physical artifacts.

Collaborators

  • SimentIT
  • Canadian Science and Technology Museum (CSTM), Ottawa

Funding

  • NSERC Collaborative Research and Development Grant (CRD) — $450K
